title: Translation Glossary Bundle
description: Tenant-scoped translation glossary bundle.  Used by  endpoints (9-12) to import / export per-tenant term overrides and to pin a frozen `glossary_hash` into TRANSLATION_APPLIED custody events.  All entries within a single bundle share a (source_lang, target_lang) pair.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this glossary format.

glossary_id.description: Stable identifier for this glossary bundle.  When omitted the API computes a UUIDv4 on save.  Used for audit-trail attribution.

tenant_id.description: Tenant scope for these entries.  Glossaries are never shared across tenants.

source_lang.description: BCP-47 source language tag.  All entries match this tag.

target_lang.description: BCP-47 target language tag.  All entries match this tag.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the glossary's purpose.

glossary_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest computed over the canonical sorted entries.  Pinned into TRANSLATION_APPLIED custody events so the audit trail can prove which glossary was applied.  Lower-case hex, 64 chars.

entries.description: Term-override entries.  Order is preserved on save; runtime applies in priority order then by entry id.

term_id.description: Stable identifier for this entry.  Server-assigned on POST.

source_term.description: Source-side substring (literal) or regex pattern (when is_regex=true).

target_term.description: Replacement string in the target language.  For regex entries this may contain back-references (\1 .. \9) that re.sub will expand.

case_sensitive.description: When true the literal/regex match is case-sensitive.

is_regex.description: When true source_term is interpreted as a Python regex (per ocr_local.translation.glossary).

priority.description: Lower priority numbers apply first.  Ties broken by stable entry id.

notes.description: Optional reviewer-facing note (not used by the runtime).
